---
title: "Model Registry"
subtitle: "Log, deploy, and serve R models"
---

## Overview

Training a model in R is only half the MLOps story. Production needs **versioned artifacts**, **metadata**, **reproducible deployment**, and **governed inference** — the same way application code uses git and CI/CD, not a shared folder of `.rds` files.

The Snowflake **Model Registry** is the in-account system of record for ML models: each **version** stores artifacts, metrics, lineage links, and deployment configuration. Python teams call `snowflake-ml-python` directly; **`snowflakeR`** lets R teams **`sfr_log_model()`** after training with `lm`, tidymodels, forecast, or custom fit objects — without authoring Python serving code.

The architectural constraint: Snowflake's serving runtime is **Python-first**. snowflakeR closes the gap by auto-generating a **CustomModel** wrapper that loads your `.rds` and runs predict via **rpy2** inside **SPCS** containers at inference time.

See [MLOps on Snowflake](../15_mlops_on_snowflake/index.qmd) for lifecycle context and [Feature Store](../17_feature_store/index.qmd) for training data and lineage inputs.

## Learning Objectives

- Explain registry concepts and the train → log → deploy → infer lifecycle
- Describe the CustomModel + rpy2 serving path
- Log models with schema contracts and optional lineage from Feature Store
- Choose inference patterns (local, in-account, REST, batch, SQL)
- Avoid deploy failures (conda deps, schema mismatch, version promotion)

---

## The problem the Registry solves {#sec-problem}

| Without registry | With registry |
|------------------|---------------|
| `model_v3_final.RData` on a laptop | Immutable **V1, V2, V3** in Snowflake |
| Unknown which data trained the model | **Lineage** to datasets and feature views |
| Hand-built Plumber/ Docker per model | **SPCS deployment** from same API |
| No audit trail of metrics | **Metrics** stored per version |
| Production points at "whatever was last saved" | **Aliases** (`@champion`) for controlled promotion |

The Registry does not replace **experiment tracking** ([Experiments](../19_experiments/index.qmd)) — it holds **promoted** models ready for serve and monitor.

---

## Core concepts {#sec-concepts}

| Concept | Definition |
|---------|------------|
| **Registry** | Schema/database holding models (`sfr_model_registry()`) |
| **Model name** | Logical identifier (`CHURN_MODEL`) — container for versions |
| **Version** | Immutable snapshot (`V1`, `V2`, …) — artifacts + metadata |
| **ModelVersion** | R handle returned by `sfr_log_model()` |
| **CustomModel** | Python class Snowflake executes in SPCS |
| **Stage artifacts** | `.rds` model + generated wrapper code |
| **input_cols / output_cols** | Schema contract for inference validation |
| **Alias** | Named pointer (`production`, `champion`) to a version |
| **Deployment** | SPCS **inference service** (HTTPS endpoint) |
| **Lineage** | Upstream: tables, feature views, datasets; downstream: deployments |
| **Batch inference** | `sfr_run_batch()` / SQL — score many rows without REST |

---

## Lifecycle overview {#sec-lifecycle}

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  subgraph dev [Development]
    TR[Train in R]
    LOC[sfr_predict_local]
  end

  subgraph reg [Registry]
    LOG[sfr_log_model]
    MET[Metrics lineage]
    VER[V1 V2 versions]
  end

  subgraph prod [Production]
    DEP[sfr_deploy_model]
    SVC[SPCS service]
    INF[sfr_predict / REST / SQL]
    MON[Monitoring]
  end

  TR --> LOC --> LOG --> MET --> VER
  VER --> DEP --> SVC --> INF --> MON
```

| Phase | What you do | Compute |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| **Train** | Fit model on Feature Store dataset or table | R in notebook / local |
| **Validate** | `sfr_predict_local()` — same logic as serve | R only |
| **Log** | `sfr_log_model()` — upload artifacts + metadata | Snowflake ML API |
| **Deploy** | `sfr_deploy_model()` — create/update SPCS service | SPCS compute pool |
| **Infer** | `sfr_predict()`, REST, SQL, batch | SPCS and/or warehouse |
| **Monitor** | Inference log + drift jobs | Warehouse + registry API |

---

## How logging and serving work {#sec-how-log}

```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
  participant R as R train session
  participant SFR as snowflakeR
  participant ST as Internal stage
  participant REG as Model Registry
  participant SPCS as SPCS inference container

  R->>SFR: sfr_log_model(model, input_cols, ...)
  SFR->>SFR: saveRDS model
  SFR->>SFR: Generate Python CustomModel + rpy2 handler
  SFR->>ST: Upload artifacts
  SFR->>REG: Register ModelVersion V1

  Note over REG,SPCS: sfr_deploy_model()
  REG->>SPCS: Create inference service

  Note over SPCS: Predict request arrives
  SPCS->>SPCS: Python CustomModel.load_context()
  SPCS->>SPCS: rpy2 loads R + readRDS
  SPCS->>SPCS: predict(model, input_df)
  SPCS-->>SPCS: Return scores as DataFrame
```

**At log time (R session):**

1. Serialize model to **`.rds`**
2. Generate **CustomModel** Python module (you do not write this)
3. Upload to **stage**; register **ModelVersion** with schema and metadata

**At inference time (SPCS container):**

1. Python handler receives batch of input rows
2. **rpy2** embeds R, loads `.rds`, calls your **`predict`** path (or custom `predict_body`)
3. Results return as a columnar structure Snowflake serves to callers

::: {.callout-note title="rpy2 vs reticulate"}
- **reticulate** (R→Python): `sfr_log_model()` calls Snowflake ML Python SDK from R  
- **rpy2** (Python→R): SPCS CustomModel calls back into R at predict time  

Same bridges as [Architecture](../02_architecture/index.qmd), reversed at serve time.
:::

---

## Setup {#sec-setup}

```r
library(snowflakeR)
conn <- sfr_connect()
conn <- sfr_load_notebook_config(conn)

reg <- sfr_model_registry(conn, database = "ML_DB", schema = "MODELS")
```

Pass **`reg`** or **`conn`** to registry functions. Explicit `sfr_model_registry()` avoids ambiguity when session database/schema differ from where models should live.

---

## Train and validate locally {#sec-train}

**Always** run predict logic locally before registration — catches schema and shape errors without SPCS deploy cycles:

```r
model <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl, data = mtcars)

test_data <- data.frame(
  wt  = c(2.5, 3.0),
  hp  = c(110, 150),
  cyl = c(4, 6)
)

preds <- sfr_predict_local(model, test_data)
```

`sfr_predict_local()` executes pure R — no Python bridge. It should match what the CustomModel will call inside the container (modulo package versions on conda-forge).

**tidymodels:** pass the fitted **workflow** object to `sfr_log_model()` — the package extracts the parsnip fit and predict interface.

---

## Log the model {#sec-log}

```r
mv <- sfr_log_model(
  reg,
  model       = model,
  model_name  = "MTCARS_MPG",
  input_cols  = list(wt = "double", hp = "double", cyl = "integer"),
  output_cols = list(prediction = "double"),
  comment     = "LM predicting MPG"
)
```

### Schema contract (`input_cols` / `output_cols`)

Inference validates incoming rows against **`input_cols`** — names and types must match scoring data. Snowflake types map from R:

| Registry type | R column type |
|---------------|---------------|
| `"double"` | numeric |
| `"integer"` | integer |
| `"string"` | character |
| `"boolean"` | logical |

Mismatch at deploy or predict time is a common production failure — define columns from the same frame used in `sfr_predict_local()`.

### Serving dependencies

SPCS inference images install R packages from **conda-forge** (plus `r-base`, `rpy2`). Declare packages needed at predict time:

```r
mv <- sfr_log_model(
  reg,
  model        = arima_fit,
  model_name   = "SALES_FORECAST",
  predict_pkgs = c("forecast"),
  conda_deps   = c("r-forecast"),
  input_cols   = ...,
  output_cols  = ...
)
```

::: {.callout-warning}
CRAN-only packages with no conda-forge recipe may **fail at deploy**. Options: custom inference image, `conda_deps`, or rewrite predict with base R — see [Appendix C](../appendices/C_troubleshooting/index.qmd).
:::

### Custom predict bodies {#sec-predict-body}

Standard `predict(model, newdata)` works for many models. **forecast**, **bsts**, and multi-column outputs need custom code:

```r
my_forecast <- function(model, input) {
  pred <- forecast::forecast(model, h = nrow(input))
  data.frame(
    point_forecast = as.numeric(pred$mean),
    lower_95       = as.numeric(pred$lower[, 2]),
    upper_95       = as.numeric(pred$upper[, 2])
  )
}

mv <- sfr_log_model(
  reg,
  model        = arima_model,
  model_name   = "SALES_FORECAST",
  predict_pkgs = c("forecast"),
  predict_body = sfr_predict_body(my_forecast),
  input_cols   = list(period = "integer"),
  output_cols  = list(
    point_forecast = "double",
    lower_95       = "double",
    upper_95       = "double"
  )
)
```

`sfr_predict_body()` converts your R function into the template the container executes — test the R function locally first, then wrap.

---

## Lineage from Feature Store {#sec-lineage}

When training data comes from Feature Store **Datasets**, pass **`training_dataset`** to complete the chain visible in Snowsight:

> **Source table → Feature View → Dataset → Model**

```r
training <- sfr_generate_dataset(
  fs,
  name     = "CHURN_TRAINING",
  spine    = "SELECT customer_id, label FROM labels",
  features = list(list(name = "CUSTOMER_FEATURES", version = "v1")),
  version  = "v1",
  spine_label_cols = "label"
)

model <- glm(label ~ ., data = training, family = binomial)

mv <- sfr_log_model(
  reg,
  model            = model,
  model_name       = "CHURN_MODEL",
  input_cols       = sfr_input_cols(training, exclude = "label"),
  output_cols      = list(prediction = "double"),
  training_dataset = training
)
```

Inspect lineage after logging:

```r
sfr_model_lineage(reg, "CHURN_MODEL", "V1", direction = "upstream")
```

---

## Metrics and version management {#sec-metrics}

```r
sfr_set_model_metric(reg, "MTCARS_MPG", "V1", "rmse", 2.45)
sfr_set_model_metric(reg, "MTCARS_MPG", "V1", "r_squared", 0.87)
sfr_show_model_metrics(reg, "MTCARS_MPG", "V1")

sfr_show_models(reg)
sfr_show_model_versions(reg, "MTCARS_MPG")
```

Log a new version when retraining:

```r
model_v2 <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl + disp, data = mtcars)

mv2 <- sfr_log_model(
  reg,
  model        = model_v2,
  model_name   = "MTCARS_MPG",
  version_name = "V2",
  input_cols   = list(wt = "double", hp = "double", cyl = "integer", disp = "double"),
  output_cols  = list(prediction = "double"),
  comment      = "V2: added displacement"
)

sfr_set_default_model_version(reg, "MTCARS_MPG", "V2")
```

---

## Aliases and promotion {#sec-aliases}

**Aliases** decouple production callers from version numbers:

```r
sfr_set_model_alias(reg, "MTCARS_MPG", "V2", "production")
sfr_set_model_alias(reg, "MTCARS_MPG", "V1", "staging")

sfr_unset_model_alias(reg, "MTCARS_MPG", "production")
```

Downstream SQL inference and REST clients can target **`@production`** while you register **V3** as challenger — promote by moving the alias, not rewriting pipelines.

---

## Deploy to SPCS {#sec-deploy}

Deployment materializes an **inference service** on a **compute pool**:

```r
sfr_deploy_model(
  reg,
  model_name   = "MTCARS_MPG",
  version_name = "V2",
  service_name = "mpg_service",
  compute_pool = "ML_POOL",
  image_repo   = "ML_DB.MODELS.ML_IMAGES"
)
```

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
  REG[Registry ModelVersion]
  IMG[Inference image conda plus rpy2]
  POOL[Compute pool]
  SVC[Inference service HTTPS]
  APP[Apps Shiny REST clients]

  REG --> SVC
  IMG --> SVC
  POOL --> SVC
  SVC --> APP
```

**Operational notes:**

- Inference uses **conda-forge** R packages baked into the service image — align with `predict_pkgs`
- Monitor via `SYSTEM$GET_SERVICE_LOGS` and service status SQL
- **Cycle compute pool** after image updates ([Production checklist](../appendices/D_production_checklist/index.qmd))
- Cold starts depend on pool sizing — consider min instances for latency-sensitive apps

List and manage services via registry helpers (`sfr_show_services`, etc. — see vignette).

---

## Inference patterns {#sec-infer}

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  Q{Who calls predict?}
  Q -->|Same R session dev| LOC[sfr_predict_local]
  Q -->|R session in Snowflake| RREG[sfr_predict]
  Q -->|External app| REST[sfr_predict_rest]
  Q -->|SQL team| SQL[sfr_predict_sql warehouse models]
  Q -->|Batch millions| BATCH[sfr_run_batch many-model]

  LOC --> RONLY[R only no deploy]
  RREG --> SPCS1[SPCS or in-session bridge]
  REST --> SPCS2[SPCS HTTPS plus PAT]
  SQL --> WH[Warehouse]
  BATCH --> SPCS3[SPCS batch job]
```

| Pattern | API | When |
|---------|-----|------|
| **Development** | `sfr_predict_local()` | Before log; no deploy |
| **In-account R batch** | `sfr_predict(reg, data)` | Scoring via Snowpark DataFrame in Snowflake |
| **Deployed service** | `sfr_predict(..., service_name=)` | After `sfr_deploy_model()` |
| **REST (external)** | `sfr_predict_rest()` | Posit Connect Shiny, apps outside Workspace |
| **Warehouse SQL** | `sfr_predict_sql()` | `target_platforms = "WAREHOUSE"` models |
| **Many-model batch** | `sfr_run_batch()` | Partition-keyed models — [Many-Model](../21_many_model/index.qmd) |

```r
new_cars <- sfr_query(conn, "SELECT wt, hp, cyl FROM car_data LIMIT 100")
scores   <- sfr_predict(reg, "MTCARS_MPG", new_cars)
```

### Workspace vs REST auth

- **`sfr_predict()`** routes through the Snowpark session — no PAT in notebooks  
- **`sfr_predict_rest()`** hits SPCS ingress directly — typically requires **PAT** outside Workspace ([R Cells §PAT](../08_r_cells_and_interop/index.qmd#sec-pat))

### Posit Connect

Publish Shiny or Quarto to **Posit Connect**; the app calls **`sfr_predict_rest()`** against the Snowflake endpoint. Registry stays authoritative; Connect is the UI layer ([MLOps](../15_mlops_on_snowflake/index.qmd)).

---

## Many-model registration {#sec-many-model}

Thousands of partition-specific models (one `.rds` per SKU) do not require thousands of registry entries. **`sfr_log_many_model()`** registers one **aggregator** CustomModel with a `model_index.json` mapping partition keys to stage paths — registration time stays ~constant. Batch scoring uses **`sfr_run_batch()`**.

See [Many-Model Patterns](../21_many_model/index.qmd) and [Parallel doSnowflake](../22_parallel_dosnowflake/index.qmd).

---

## Experiments and monitoring {#sec-ops}

| Concern | Tool |
|---------|------|
| Hyperparameter search, run comparison | [Experiments](../19_experiments/index.qmd) — log before promoting to registry |
| Production drift / performance | [Model monitoring](../20_model_monitoring/index.qmd) — attach to deployed version |
| Parallel retrain | doSnowflake + log new version |

Typical flow: many runs in **Experiments** → pick winner → **`sfr_log_model()`** → deploy → **monitor**.

---

## Common pitfalls {#sec-pitfalls}

| Issue | Mitigation |
|-------|------------|
| Deploy fails — missing R package | `predict_pkgs`, `conda_deps`, custom image |
| Column name / type mismatch | Match `input_cols`; test with `sfr_predict_local()` |
| Wrong output shape | Use `predict_body`; forecast models need data.frame contract |
| Stale production version | Aliases + `sfr_set_default_model_version()` |
| REST fails from notebook | Use `sfr_predict()` in Workspace; PAT for external REST |
| High latency | Warm service; right-size compute pool |

---

## Companion artifacts {#sec-companion}

| Resource | Content |
|----------|---------|
| Vignette `model-registry` | Deploy params, SQL predict, export |
| `workspace_model_registry.ipynb` | Workspace walkthrough |
| `workspace_forecasting_demo.ipynb` | forecast + `predict_body` example |

---

## Next steps {#sec-next}

[Experiments](../19_experiments/index.qmd) · [Model monitoring](../20_model_monitoring/index.qmd) · [End-to-end pipeline](../23_end_to_end/index.qmd)
